response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack software engineer (web, mobile, embedded tooling) with strong testing discipline and some low-latency/high-throughput backend work, but the role requires hardware-centric expertise the resume does not demonstrate: SoC/IP modeling, AI-accelerator performance analysis, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and hardware-software co-design. There is no evidence of deep-learning workload optimization or silicon/chip-level performance architecture, so the profile falls well short of the core requirements for a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: Long, broad software engineering career including low-latency/high-throughput microservices (Dunnhumby)
Some systems-level C/C++ exposure via National Instruments LabWindows/CVI and embedded work
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, and active knowledge sharing
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or hardware-software co-design background
Profile is application/web software, not principal-level hardware performance architecture
